Yaganti Uma Maheswara Temple

# Yaganti Uma Maheswara Temple

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 10, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

2

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Srisailam’s fame as one of the twelve Jyotirlingas makes it a spiritual magnet, but Yaganti carries it’s own unique aura. - The temple here is dedicated to Lord Shiva, but what truly fascinates devotees and visitors alike is the massive Nandi statue. Unlike most stone idols, this one is said to grow in size year after year. Archaeologists have studied it, and while some attribute the phenomenon to the rock’s expansion due to weathering, devotees see it as divine will.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!PJVu!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4517a8f4-3f20-4498-8771-f848d9821db4_900x499.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!PJVu!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4517a8f4-3f20-4498-8771-f848d9821db4_900x499.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Yaganti Uma Maheswara Swamy Temple, was originally intended to be for Shri Venkateswara by Agastya Maharshi. However as the toe nail of the deity was broken, it could not be installed. An upset Agastya then prayed to Shiva, who appeared before the Maharshi, and asked him to build a temple for him, here as it resembles Kailasha more. The Maharshi then requested Shiva to appear along with Parvati in a single stone, and so he is worshipped as Uma Maheswara here.

Another Sthala Purana says Shiva appeared in the form of a tiger to test his devotee Chiteppa. On seeing the tiger, he realized it was none other than Shiva himself, and exclaimed Neganti Shivanu ne kanti (I saw Shiva I saw), which explains the name of the place too.

Yaganti Uma Maheswara Temple is a jewel of Vijayanagara architecture. Bukka Raya, one of the empire’s founders, commissioned it, and you can see the hallmarks of that era in its intricate carvings, pillared halls, and balanced proportions.

The setting amplifies its drama, the temple nestles among towering hills and rocky outcrops, almost like a natural amphitheater. That backdrop makes the legends of Agastya, Chiteppa, and the ever-growing Nandi feel alive, as if the hills themselves are witnesses to the divine play.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!h3Nq!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe6753a89-7e01-4e79-b176-3dbec6f25c72_900x360.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!h3Nq!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe6753a89-7e01-4e79-b176-3dbec6f25c72_900x360.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

One of the most enchanting features of Yaganti are the Pushkarinis. These temple tanks never run dry, even in the harshest summers, because the water flows naturally from beneath the surrounding hills. It’s always fresh, cool, and crystal clear, almost as if the earth itself is offering a continuous blessing.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a2BU!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feac57946-f1b8-42f8-bddf-6ca3b9f4c8fd_550x412.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a2BU!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feac57946-f1b8-42f8-bddf-6ca3b9f4c8fd_550x412.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ek-K!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F89c403c8-5a06-452f-b07d-0a7812dc5a91_500x375.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ek-K!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F89c403c8-5a06-452f-b07d-0a7812dc5a91_500x375.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The water her flows through the mouth of Nandi into the temple tanks, turning the sacred bull into a living conduit of grace. Devotees see this as a direct blessing from Shiva himself, and so most take a dip here before entering for darshan of Uma Maheswara Swamy.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!z_v-!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F76229041-d24b-4735-8649-0444b5d81c03_800x1067.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!z_v-!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F76229041-d24b-4735-8649-0444b5d81c03_800x1067.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

There are two famous caves at Yaganti, one is the Agastya cave, where aharshi Agastya is believed to have performed penance to Shiva. The approach itself is dramatic, 120 stone steps leading upward, almost like a pilgrimage within the pilgrimage. Entering the cave feels like stepping into the Maharshi’s solitude, where devotion carved out its own sanctum.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!joVj!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7013f068-d28e-4206-9d2c-1c9ab43aec6f_512x269.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!joVj!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7013f068-d28e-4206-9d2c-1c9ab43aec6f_512x269.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2iDI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1c44d154-dd41-4889-8e66-8e84fac5ce22_800x600.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2iDI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1c44d154-dd41-4889-8e66-8e84fac5ce22_800x600.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Another is Venkateswara Cave, that houses the original damaged murti of Shri Venkateswara, the one Agastya intended to consecrate before destiny redirected the temple toward Shiva.

There is a small cave, where Pothuluri Veerabrahmendra Swamy, the famed seer, is believed to have composed portions of his Kala Gnaanam (prophecies about the future) right there, in that secluded space.

The Nandi at Yaganti is truly its most iconic feature. Unlike any other Nandi, this one is believed to be growing in size year after year. The phenomenon is so striking that over time, some of the surrounding pillars had to be removed to make space for it. Devotees perform pradakshina around the Nandi as part of their visit, making it both a ritual centerpiece and a living mystery.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bAKd!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc95996ec-e75e-4b81-8607-1aa260417c26_912x608.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bAKd!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc95996ec-e75e-4b81-8607-1aa260417c26_912x608.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!htOp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F62c65a52-2962-44b6-9b58-2e05b8d68b75_800x448.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!htOp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F62c65a52-2962-44b6-9b58-2e05b8d68b75_800x448.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

As per the Kaala Gnaanam of Brahmam Garu, at the end of Kali Yuga, the Nandi will reach its largest size and then come to life, letting out a mighty roar.

Just like Kotappa Konda, crows are never seen here. As per the *Sthala Purana* when Agastya Maharshi was performing penance, crows disturbed his meditation. In anger, he cursed them never to enter Yaganti.

Also since Shani’s vahana is a crow, it is believed that Shani himself cannot enter the temple. That makes Yaganti not only a place of divine presence but also a sanctuary shielded from Shani himself.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
